label: Virtue as Sole Good
description: Marcus stresses that moral goodness and active justice are the only source of true profit, rendering external things like wealth and pleasure completely indifferent.

label: Cites Platonic Dialogues
description: This link connects historical annotations of Platonic Dialogues with Marcus's reflections, illustrating the deep intellectual tradition that shaped his Stoic practice.

label: Aligning with Providence
description: Marcus reminds himself to accept fate willingly as a necessary part of the rational cosmic design (Logos), trusting that whatever universal nature provides is beneficial.

label: Sovereignty of Judgment
description: These passages emphasize the Stoic practice of controlling opinions, arguing that external events have no power over the soul unless we assent to a judgment of harm.

label: Cites Platonic Dialogues
description: This link connects historical annotations of Platonic Dialogues with Marcus's reflections, illustrating the deep intellectual tradition that shaped his Stoic practice.

label: Rejoining the Cosmic Body
description: Marcus explains that a human who separates himself from society can rejoin it by aligning with the divine providence that governs the whole.

label: Renouncing Fame Through Opinion
description: Marcus looks down on human vanity, urging himself to cast away the desire for fame, which is just a false opinion of others.

label: Time Limits of Ancient Sages
description: Marcus meditates on the short lives of ancient sages, warning himself that his own limit of time is quickly passing away.

label: Allaying Soul Distempers
description: Marcus notes that the time to calm his soul is limited, urging himself to immediately submit to providence and live according to nature.

label: Examining Universal Mind
description: Marcus instructs examining the universal mind to understand that he is a cooperative part of a rationally ordered whole.
